compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can run a failing test suite for create-dso-app.sh before implementation begins
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
TDD Gate Story — must be RED before any implementation story (S2–S7) may begin.

Done Definition:
- tests/scripts/test-create-dso-app.sh exists and is executable (chmod +x applied at creation)
- Running bash tests/scripts/test-create-dso-app.sh fails with clear FAIL output before any implementation code exists
- Test suite covers: expected project structure (package.json, app/ or pages/ dir, DSO infrastructure files present), project name substitution (including sanitized special chars), presence of .dso-init-complete sentinel, exit code 0 on newline acknowledgment, exit code 1 on stdin close/EOF
- External binaries (claude, brew, git, npm) are all stubbed via PATH-prepended mock bin directory; claude stub accepts expected args and exits 0 without launching real application
- Tests confirm idempotency check: exit 0 + informative message when .dso-init-complete already present in target dir
- All test assertions fail (RED) before any implementation stories begin

Considerations:
- Mock all external binaries via export PATH="$MOCK_BIN_DIR:$PATH" before invoking create-dso-app.sh to ensure tests are hermetic and can run in CI without real Homebrew or Claude Code installed
- Stub scripts must be minimal and deterministic: accept the exact args the installer will call them with, exit 0, and produce no side effects

Escalation Policy: Escalate when blocked
